expectations.text: Creates Named Ranges for input cells FIRST before rewriting any formulas (dependency ordering)
expectations.evidence: 'Named Ranges Added' section shows all assumption cells given named ranges before formulas were converted. Input ranges (Revenue_Base, Revenue_Growth, COGS_Pct, etc.) listed before the formula conversions.

expectations.text: Proposes Named Range names following IDFA convention (Inp_ prefix for inputs)
expectations.evidence: Named ranges use generic names without Inp_ prefix: Revenue_Base, Revenue_Growth, COGS_Pct, Equity, Debt, Total_Capital, etc. Does not follow IDFA Inp_ convention.

expectations.text: Rewrites formulas ONE at a time, not all at once
expectations.evidence: Three separate formula sections: 'Formula 1: C6 (Revenue Y1)', 'Formula 2: D6 (Revenue Y2)', 'Formula 3: E6 (Revenue Y3)'. Each documented individually.

expectations.text: Validates that outputs match the original values after each formula rewrite
expectations.evidence: Validation Summary: 'Formula 1: PASS — Revenue_Base resolves to $B$2 = 10,000,000', 'Formula 2: PASS — Revenue_Growth resolves to $B$3 = 0.10', 'Formula 3: PASS'. States 'All 3 conversions validated: numbers unchanged.'

expectations.text: Does NOT delete and rebuild the model from scratch — retrofits in place
expectations.evidence: 'Remaining Formulas (6 of 9, unconverted)' table shows in-place retrofit with 3 done, 6 remaining. The model is not rebuilt from scratch.

expectations.text: Uses the idfa-ops create-range command to create Named Ranges programmatically
expectations.evidence: Method states: 'Manual conversion using openpyxl (no skill files used)'. Named ranges were created via openpyxl directly, not via idfa_ops.py create-range.
